title: Alaskan's flip and twist nails X Games gold
summary: Dane Ferguson, 28, from Anchorage won the Snowmobile Next Trick competition at the Winter X Games 13 in Aspen, Colo., landing a trick he calls a Twist Off -- a backward flip with a twist.

title: Police investigate Rothe, Lekanoff deaths (7/7/04)
summary: Anchorage police said Tuesday that they have identified the homicide victim whose torso was found a little over a year ago on the shore at Beluga Point. The body, missing its head and legs, was that of Desiree Michelle Lekanoff, an Anchorage woman who had been reported missing in December 2001, police said. Lekanoff, 5 feet tall and weighing 100 pounds, would have been 23 in April 2002, said her mother, Antonina Lekanoff, who lives in Maine. Police do not know how Desiree Lekanoff died, how long she may have been in Turnagain Arm, or whether she was dismembered intentionally or by some natural process, said Capt. Tom Nelson, a department spokesman. Nor do investigators know whether Lekanoff's homicide is linked to the killing of Michelle T. Rothe, a 32-year-old Anchorage woman whose torso …

title: Palin campaigns for Republican in  Georgia's runoff
summary: ATLANTA -- Republican Sen. Saxby Chambliss enlisted Gov. Sarah Palin to rally conservatives while Democratic challenger Jim Martin pushed to activate black voters, as they grapple for advantage in a Tuesday runoff that will shape Democrats' hold on power in Washington. Palin, the Alaska governor who was John McCain's vice presidential running mate, attended private fundraisers Sunday night and was set to speak at rallies across the state today. McCain carried Georgia with 52 percent of the vote on Nov. 4. Martin planned to campaign with prominent Georgia Democrats, including U.S. Rep. John Lewis, as he sought to rekindle the strong showing by African American voters in the general election that President-elect Barack Obama sparked. Neither Chambliss nor Martin crossed the 50 percent thresh…

title: Police say girlfriend assisted fugitive
summary: FAIRBANKS -- Fairbanks police have arrested a woman accused of helping her boyfriend remain a fugitive for more than two months. Aurora Celeste Cox, 21, is charged with hindering prosecution and permitting an unauthorized person to drive. Prosecutors say Cox helped 29-year-old Robert Tyrone Gentleman III when he was on the lam. Prosecutors say Gentleman used Cox's Chevrolet Cavalier to outrun police twice in August and September. Gentleman was arrested in October after a four-hour standoff. He's being held on charges of robbery, assault, driving with a suspended license, failure to provide notice of an accident and reckless driving.

title: Mo. woman pleads guilty in videotaped sex killing
summary: A woman struck a plea deal Monday but still faces more charges in a crime spree that included the videotaped sexual torture and slaying of another woman. Dena D. Riley, 42, pleaded guilty to first-degree murder, kidnapping, sodomy and sex abuse in the May 2006 attack on Marsha Spicer, who was suffocated during sex at an apartment that Riley shared with her boyfriend. Riley also pleaded guilty to kidnapping, sodomy, sex abuse and assault in an attack on Michelle Huff-Ricci, 36, of Kansas City. Prosecutors said Riley and her boyfriend, Richard Davis, videotaped themselves assaulting the women to fulfill Davis' violent sexual fantasies. Earlier this year, Davis, was convicted and sentenced to death for Spicer's slaying. In return for her plea, Riley avoided the death penalty in Spicer's killi…

title: Armed men rob Taco Bell
summary: Anchorage police are looking for three men who robbed a Taco Bell on Northern Lights Boulevard early Monday morning. The men, described as Pacific Islanders wearing dark clothing and bandanas over their faces, entered the store at about 1 a.m. when an employee opened a door to get supplies from a shed, according to a statement from the Anchorage Police Department. One of the men was armed with a rifle and the other two were carrying handguns, according to witnesses. They forced the employees into the walk-in cooler and took money from the registers, police said. A witness saw them running south from the Taco Bell. Anyone with information about this incident should call police dispatch at 786-8900 or Crime Stoppers at 561-STOP.

title: Bouldering hotspots, photos and videos
summary: Below are some of the more popular bouldering sites in Southcentral. Like the climbers themselves, Alaska sites are a bit different than those in the Lower 48. "In the states," said veteran Alaska climber Todd Helgeson, "bouldering attracts a lot of people from the city who aren't comfortable in the wilderness. Here, you have to be more adventurous. "In the Lower 48, you've got your guidebook, chalk, trails around the boulders. Many places are almost manicured. We don't have any areas that are civilized like that here in Alaska. If you want to climb at Ptarmigan, for instance, you have to hike or mountain bike to get there and venture out a little farther." Weiner Lake: Also know as Wookie Wall, this extensive collection of small crags and boulders is favored because of good southern expos…

title: Man arrested after two drive-by shootings
summary: A 28-year-old man was arrested Tuesday morning after two drive-by shootings in East Anchorage. No one was injured in either incident. Anchorage police say Christopher Taong, a felon on federal probation, shot at the two houses because of an unpaid drug debt. The first shooting occurred just after 1 a.m. in the 3600 block of Aldren Circle. Five people were in the house when a man in a Dodge Durango opened fire on it, police said. About 10 minutes later, the gunman drove by another home belonging to the same family and opened fire. Eight people were in that home at the time, most of them children, police said. The family told police that they knew Taong had threatened one of their younger family members over the drug debt. They said they had noticed a suspicious white Dodge Durango in the ne…

title: Parks Department marks solstice with Run for the Sun
summary: Finally, we've hit bottom. After ringing in solstice at 3:04 this morning, sun worshipers can at least offer thanks they're not trying to use solar power to stay warm in Fairbanks. Last year, researchers with the Cold Climate Housing Research Center there looked into how much oomph sun's power could deliver to four tracking arrays of photovoltaic solar panels on Dec. 21. "That was the whole idea," said center president Jack Hebert. "Why not start this thing on the coldest, darkest day of the year?" The conditions: 35 degrees below zero, thick ice fog, sunrise at nearly 11 a.m. The result: Not enough electricity to power one incandescent light bulb from arrays that, at peak performance, can power 10 homes. Today Fairbanks will bask in three hours, 41 minutes of sunlight. But don't cry for r…

title: Point Thomson lease fight hits peak
summary: More than 30 years ago, drillers made a spectacular discovery of oil and natural gas at a remote site called Point Thomson, along the Arctic coast of Alaska. Many observers figured the new field would soon add to the production from an even richer North Slope deposit, Prudhoe Bay, which started sending oil down the trans-Alaska pipeline the same year as the Point Thomson find, 1977. But it hasn't worked out that way. Point Thomson has been dormant all these years. The 106,201-acre field has no working wells. It hasn't produced a single barrel of oil or molecule of gas. Now the oil companies that long ago leased acreage at Point Thomson are in jeopardy of eviction because the landlord, the state, is tired of waiting for the taxable oil and gas to start flowing. State officials have canceled…

title: Man is sent to jail for failure to pay support
summary: In an uncommon criminal trial this week, a jury convicted a man for refusing to pay child support and a judge sentenced him to a month in jail. He'll also have to pay more than $100,000 in fines and back support and will be on probation for 10 years. Lawrence Belarde, an Anchorage concrete contractor, begins his jail term Monday, said prosecuting attorney Jake Metcalfe. Belarde's attorney declined to comment. Belarde was charged with criminal failure to pay the support for about 14 months in 2006 and 2007, a misdemeanor. According to a state formula, he was required to pay about $500 a month to support his son, who is now 14. But Metcalfe said he'd been ignoring his obligation for much longer than that. "He's not paid a dime since the kid was born," Metcalfe said. The state garnished his P…

title: Manhunt ends at Fred Meyer
summary: Anchorage police arrested a 22-year-old local fugitive after he was seen shopping at a Fred Meyer store Tuesday evening. The arrest ended a monthlong manhunt for Phonesavanh Vongthongdy, who was wanted on felony warrants for drug conspiracy, illegal possession of machine guns and other drug-related charges, police said. Somebody tipped off the police after seeing him at the Midtown Fred Meyer store on Northern Lights Boulevard shortly after 5 p.m. Tuesday. Police said they were able to identify him from the store's security camera system and plainclothes officers who entered the store. When Vongthongdy left the store, officers arrested him and his companion, Brodrick Tucker, without incident, police said. Tucker was wanted on a parole violation. After the arrest, police said, they discover…
